title: 52-Week High Proximity (Anchoring Bias)
idea: Investors anchor on the 52-week high and under-react to good news near it, so price keeps drifting up (George & Hwang 2004, J. of Finance). The effect is cross-sectional — weak on one isolated series.
signal_rule: Enter long when close ≥ 95% of the 52-week high and rising; exit below 90%.

trend_gbm.regime: Geometric Brownian Motion, +15%/yr drift — a persistent uptrend.
trend_gbm.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe -0.54 (CAGR -6.0%) — a adverse regime for this edge; the thesis makes no strong prediction here. Out-of-sample, 0% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

mean_rev_ou.regime: Ornstein–Uhlenbeck process oscillating around 100 — pure mean reversion.
mean_rev_ou.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe -0.30 (CAGR -3.7%) — a adverse regime for this edge; this matches what the thesis predicts. Out-of-sample, 0% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

regime_switch.regime: 200-bar blocks alternating trend and mean reversion — punishes regime overfitting.
regime_switch.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe -0.07 (CAGR -1.6%) — a marginal regime for this edge; the thesis makes no strong prediction here. Out-of-sample, 0% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

fat_tail.regime: Zero-drift Student-t(3) returns — extreme daily moves ~2–4× more often than normal.
fat_tail.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe -0.16 (CAGR -1.6%) — a adverse regime for this edge; this matches what the thesis predicts. Out-of-sample, 0% of walk-forward folds were profitable.
